arts.ttl: This Week In Lazio History: January 20-26
arts.sum: This week we remember some important games, Gottardi’s goal in the derby, a demolition of Milan and a rare win against Juve, plus a happy birthday to Giorgio Chinaglia. Matches of the Week Date: Sunday, January 20, 2002 Venue: Stadio Olimpico, Rome Fixture: Lazio Perugia 5-0 The Biancocelesti easily demolish Perugia despite a hostile home […] The post This Week In Lazio History: January 20-26 appeared first on The Laziali.

arts.ttl: Lazio vs. Fiorentina: a six-pointer for Europe
arts.sum: Fiorentina have won their last two league games against Lazio (in February and September 2024, both at the Franchi by 2-1); the last time the Viola achieved more consecutive wins against the Biancocelesti in Serie A was between December 2006 and March 2008 (four in that case). Lazio are unbeaten in their last eight home league games against Fiorentina (W6 D2), while the Viola`s last away win against the Biancocelesti in Serie A was back on 15th May 2016 (4-2 under Paulo Sousa).

arts.ttl: Lazio Win Race for Casadei: Transfer Details Revealed
arts.sum: Lazio have emerged victorious in a lengthy and competitive pursuit of Cesare Casadei from Chelsea, bringing the talented midfielder back to Italian football. The 22-year-old, who has struggled to find playing time in the Premier League, will join Marco Baroni's team after an intricate negotiation process. arts.ttl: Nuno Tavares Sidelined: Lazio Defender to Miss Three Matches
arts.sum: Lazio's Portuguese defender Nuno Tavares is set to be out of action for approximately ten days due to an injury sustained during Wednesday's match against Real Sociedad. The official verdict on the extent of his left adductor injury will be confirmed today following medical examinations.
